quality.rationale: 需综合Ricci恒等式、速度梯度张量正交分解及投影算子性质进行多步张量推导，逻辑链条完整且为黑洞奇点理论核心。

origin.question: [文件 1: 1_s14_14.10.md]Problem 14.10. If $\theta$ is the expansion of a fluid, derive the Raychaudhuri equation$$\frac {\mathrm {d} \theta}{\mathrm {d} r} = a_{; a}^{\alpha} + 2 \omega^{2} - 2 \sigma^{2} - \frac {1}{3} \theta^{2} - R_{\alpha \beta} u^{\alpha} u^{\beta}$$where $\omega^2 = \frac{1}{2}\omega_\alpha\beta\omega^\alpha\beta$ , $\sigma^2 = \frac{1}{2}\sigma_\alpha\beta\sigma^\alpha\beta$ , and the notation is the same as in Problem 5.18.## SolutionSolution 14.10. From the definition of $\theta = \nabla \cdot \mathbf{u}$ we have$$\begin{array}{l} \frac {\mathrm {d} \theta}{\mathrm {d} \tau} = u^{\beta} \left(u_{; \alpha}^{\alpha}\right); \beta = u^{\beta} u_{; \alpha \beta}^{\alpha} = u^{\beta} \left(u_{; \beta \alpha}^{\alpha} - R^{\alpha} \beta \alpha \gamma u^{\gamma}\right) \tag {1} \\ = \mathfrak {u}^{\beta} \mathfrak {u}_{; \beta \alpha}^{\alpha} - \mathrm {R}_{\beta \gamma} \mathfrak {u}^{\beta} \mathfrak {u}^{\gamma} . \end{array}$$But$$\begin{array}{l} \mathrm {u}_{; \beta \alpha}^{\alpha} \mathrm {u}^{\beta} = (\mathrm {u}_{; \beta}^{\alpha} \mathrm {u}^{\beta})_{; \alpha} - \mathrm {u}_{; \beta}^{\alpha} \mathrm {u}^{\beta}_{; \alpha} \\ = \mathrm {a}_{; \alpha}^{\alpha} - \left(\omega_{\beta}^{\alpha} + \sigma_{\beta}^{\alpha} + \frac {1}{3} \theta \mathrm {P}_{\beta}^{\alpha} - \mathrm {a}^{\alpha} \mathrm {u}_{\beta}\right) \\ \times \left(\omega^{\beta}_{\alpha} + \sigma^{\beta}_{\alpha} + \frac {1}{3} \theta \mathrm {P}^{\beta}_{\alpha} - \mathrm {a}^{\beta} \mathrm {u}_{\alpha}\right) \tag {2} \end{array}$$where we have used Problem 5.18. Using the symmetry properties of $\omega_{\alpha \beta}$ , $\sigma_{\alpha \beta}$ , and $\mathbf{P}_{\alpha \beta}$ and the fact that they are orthogonal to $\mathbf{u}$ , Equations (1) and (2) imply$$\begin{array}{l} \frac {\mathrm {d} \theta}{\mathrm {d} r} = \mathsf {a}_{; \alpha}^{\alpha} - \omega^{\alpha} \beta^{\omega \beta}_{\alpha} - \sigma^{\alpha}_{\beta} \sigma^{\beta}_{\alpha} - \frac {1}{3} \theta^{2} - \mathrm {R}_{\alpha \beta} \mathrm {u}^{\alpha} \mathrm {u}^{\beta} \\ = a_{; \alpha}^{\alpha} + 2 \omega^{2} - 2 \sigma^{2} - \frac {1}{3} \theta^{2} - R_{\alpha \beta} u^{\alpha} u^{\beta}. \end{array}$$
origin.solution: Solution 14.10. From the definition of $\theta = \nabla \cdot \mathbf{u}$ we have$$\begin{array}{l} \frac {\mathrm {d} \theta}{\mathrm {d} \tau} = u^{\beta} \left(u_{; \alpha}^{\alpha}\right); \beta = u^{\beta} u_{; \alpha \beta}^{\alpha} = u^{\beta} \left(u_{; \beta \alpha}^{\alpha} - R^{\alpha} \beta \alpha \gamma u^{\gamma}\right) \tag {1} \\ = \mathfrak {u}^{\beta} \mathfrak {u}_{; \beta \alpha}^{\alpha} - \mathrm {R}_{\beta \gamma} \mathfrak {u}^{\beta} \mathfrak {u}^{\gamma} . \end{array}$$But$$\begin{array}{l} \mathrm {u}_{; \beta \alpha}^{\alpha} \mathrm {u}^{\beta} = (\mathrm {u}_{; \beta}^{\alpha} \mathrm {u}^{\beta})_{; \alpha} - \mathrm {u}_{; \beta}^{\alpha} \mathrm {u}^{\beta}_{; \alpha} \\ = \mathrm {a}_{; \alpha}^{\alpha} - \left(\omega_{\beta}^{\alpha} + \sigma_{\beta}^{\alpha} + \frac {1}{3} \theta \mathrm {P}_{\beta}^{\alpha} - \mathrm {a}^{\alpha} \mathrm {u}_{\beta}\right) \\ \times \left(\omega^{\beta}_{\alpha} + \sigma^{\beta}_{\alpha} + \frac {1}{3} \theta \mathrm {P}^{\beta}_{\alpha} - \mathrm {a}^{\beta} \mathrm {u}_{\alpha}\right) \tag {2} \end{array}$$where we have used Problem 5.18. Using the symmetry properties of $\omega_{\alpha \beta}$ , $\sigma_{\alpha \beta}$ , and $\mathbf{P}_{\alpha \beta}$ and the fact that they are orthogonal to $\mathbf{u}$ , Equations (1) and (2) imply$$\begin{array}{l} \frac {\mathrm {d} \theta}{\mathrm {d} r} = \mathsf {a}_{; \alpha}^{\alpha} - \omega^{\alpha} \beta^{\omega \beta}_{\alpha} - \sigma^{\alpha}_{\beta} \sigma^{\beta}_{\alpha} - \frac {1}{3} \theta^{2} - \mathrm {R}_{\alpha \beta} \mathrm {u}^{\alpha} \mathrm {u}^{\beta} \\ = a_{; \alpha}^{\alpha} + 2 \omega^{2} - 2 \sigma^{2} - \frac {1}{3} \theta^{2} - R_{\alpha \beta} u^{\alpha} u^{\beta}. \end{array}$$
